topic: Magnetic decay asymmetry ratio — change-detected re-review (post-c3 rewrite: +89% detail_evidence, +110% tldr_evidence, new SAA longitude/axisymmetry argument, WIN-041/042 precedent invocation)
change_summary: detail_evidence_length grew 2988→5672 (+89.8%); detail_verdict_length 1368→1684 (+23.1%); tldr_evidence_length 264→554 (+109.8%); tldr_verdict_length 142→283 (+99.3%). Rewrite addresses c3 holes (3:1 acknowledgment, Atlantic-vs-American wording, monitor.py hedge, hemispheric-incommensurability framing). New material adds: explicit advocate-mode preempt about Olson&Deguen/Aubert fitted-vs-derived; SAA-longitude argument against axisymmetric H(r); WIN-005/041/042 re-slicing precedent. Sourced from monitor/integrity/drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10:33Z; obe_skipped=5 (WIN-052/054/055/033/057 all re-reviewed since audit, all bumped by MOND-cluster verdict change cascade).
our_argument_summary: Hemispheric magnetic decay asymmetry is explained by standard MHD geodynamo physics (reversed-flux patches at the CMB, Olson & Deguen 2012; Aubert et al. 2013) and measured by CHAOS-7. Dome asserts 3:1 ratio but provides no derivation from dome geometry, no published source for specifically 3:1, no time window, and no mechanism connecting the dome.+s axisymmetric H(r) to the SAA.+s specific longitudinal location. WIN also fails post-hoc test (CHAOS-7 was indexed public dataset before any plausible dome commit; no monitor.py domain).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py present in raw-text/ corpus; cannot verify automation surface. The page-hash byte-identical-for-34-polls evidence is consistent with no recent additions of an asymmetry-ratio domain, but the absence of a monitor.py capture means the c4 verdict text.+s +no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus+ is a hedge over an evidentiary gap, not a positive verification.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, Earth Planets Space)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome.+s WIN-063 raw text asserts 3:1 with no cited empirical source AND no derivation path. c4 text states the dome +adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation+ but the dome.+s actual paragraph does not cite CHAOS-7 as the source of 3:1 — see Hole 1 below.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: c4 detail_evidence asserts +the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph as if that act of reading were itself a derivation+ — but raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:13 shows the dome.+s actual claim is +A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow+, framed as a structural prediction of toroidal asymmetric flow, with no cited source for the 3:1 number. Attributing CHAOS-7 as the dome.+s source for 3:1 is a strawman the dome can rebut by quoting its own paragraph.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No monitor.py present in raw-text/ corpus to verify; verdict text appropriately hedged to +no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus+. Page hash byte-identical for 34 consecutive polls (per raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt header) is corroborating evidence that no recent monitor surface change has occurred for this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Hemispheric/SAA decay asymmetry is described and explained in peer-reviewed literature: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020, doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9); Olson & Deguen 2012 (doi:10.1038/ngeo1506); Aubert et al. 2013 (doi:10.1038/nature12574). Dome renames the cause (toroidal aetheric return-path through SAA shoulder) without quantitative derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 published 2020; IGRF-14 cycle published 2024 (5-yr cadence after IGRF-13 2019). Dome.+s WIN-063 dates from V51-era (2026-04 cluster). Indexed public datasets predate the dome.+s adoption — fails Part 3.1b legitimate-novelty carve-out.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula on the dome site maps disc_radius=20015 km, firmament_height=9086 km, sun_altitude=5733 km, or moon_altitude=2534 km to specifically 3:1 (rather than 2:1 or 5:1). The dome.+s only stated mechanism is qualitative (sub-terrestrial return path through SAA shoulder); H(r) is axisymmetric in longitude — see Hole 3 below.

holes_found.description: Source-attribution strawman risk. detail_evidence asserts the dome.+s 3:1 +is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph as if that act of reading were itself a derivation+, and later +adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry+. But raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:13 shows the dome.+s actual WIN-063 paragraph asserts 3:1 with NO citation of CHAOS-7 as its source — the dome frames 3:1 as a structural prediction of toroidal asymmetric core flow versus a symmetric globe dipole. A dome defender can quote the dome paragraph and our paragraph side-by-side and accuse us of fabricating a source attribution. The deeper critique (no derivation path from dome geometry, no published source either way) survives this fix — both options (post-hoc adoption from observation, or pure assertion) fail the independent-prediction test — but the current framing as-written is rebuttable.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe to dilemma form: +The dome states 3:1 without citing an empirical source for the specific value and without deriving it from any of the four canonical geometric parameters. Either the 3:1 was adopted from a published measurement (post-hoc), or it was asserted by fiat (no testable derivation); either path fails the independent-prediction test.+ This removes the source-attribution strawman while preserving the substantive critique.

holes_found.description: Verdict-vs-precedent inconsistency. detail_evidence closing paragraph invokes WIN-041 and WIN-042 (both verdict +Misleading+) as precedent for the re-slicing pattern: +Computing a ratio from one WIN.+s data does not constitute an independent prediction — this is the same pattern that earned WIN-041 and WIN-042 Misleading verdicts for re-slicing decay statistics from the same dataset.+ But WIN-063 is currently verdicted +Std Model Explains+, not +Misleading+. A dome defender will say: +By your own logic, WIN-063 should be Misleading like WIN-041/042 — pick a lane.+ Two paths to reconcile: (a) change WIN-063 verdict to +Misleading+ to match the invoked precedent; (b) soften the precedent invocation by noting that WIN-041 was Misleading for multi-station-counting and WIN-042 for threshold-duplication-of-WIN-037, while WIN-063.+s issue is computing a derived metric — same spirit, different operational pattern — and the +Std Model Explains+ verdict applies because the underlying phenomenon (hemispheric asymmetric decay) is genuinely explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics. Recommended path is (b) — the underlying-physics-is-explained reading is the cleaner verdict — but the text must reconcile the tension explicitly.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to detail_evidence after the WIN-041/042 invocation: +WIN-041 and WIN-042 earned Misleading via station-count duplication and threshold restatement respectively; WIN-063 differs in pattern but shares the family resemblance of deriving from already-confirmed data. The Std Model Explains verdict here reflects that the underlying asymmetric-decay phenomenon is independently explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics — the re-slicing concern is supplementary, not the primary verdict driver.+ Alternatively, escalate to ISS and consider verdict reclassification to +Misleading+ if the operator prefers consistency with WIN-041/042.

holes_found.description: Longitudinal-symmetry argument is correct but slightly vulnerable. detail_evidence states +no element of the published dome geometry breaks longitudinal symmetry+ in support of the SAA-longitude-incompatibility argument. This is defensible (the four canonical scalars + H(r) are all axisymmetric), but a defender could point to dynamic features outside +geometry+ that break symmetry instantaneously: the sun.+s position above the disc at any given moment, the moon.+s position, transient aetheric flow patterns. These average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales (the SAA is a long-term feature, not a daily one), so the rebuttal is weak — but the text leaves the rebuttal available by not addressing it.
holes_found.recommendation: Insert a clarifying clause: +The dome.+s static published geometry is axisymmetric in longitude, and transient features (sun position, moon position, aetheric flow) average to azimuthal symmetry over secular timescales — nothing in the published model singles out the SAA.+s specific longitudinal location as the return-path exit.+ This pre-empts the dynamic-features rebuttal.

holes_found.description: detail_evidence (b) +over what time period+ is a rhetorical question that the dome doesn.+t directly answer, but the dome.+s raw text also doesn.+t specify a time period — its claim is timescale-agnostic (+A 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow+ is a structural claim, not a rate claim). Asking +over what time period+ may not be the right attack; the actual missing piece is +over what averaging window does the 3:1 hold+, which is subtly different. A pedantic defender could say +we.+re claiming a steady-state structural ratio, not a transient rate, so the time-period question is misframed.+ This is minor because the rest of the (a)/(c)/(d) critique is strong, but (b) reads as filler.
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop (b) or rephrase to +(b) over what averaging window the 3:1 should be measured (instantaneous, decadal, secular) — and what the dome predicts that ratio should be at each window+ to sharpen the attack.

stronger_arguments.description: The dome.+s structural-incompatibility argument (+a 3:1 asymmetry is structurally incompatible with symmetric core flow+) could be turned around as a stronger critique. Asymmetric MHD geodynamo simulations (Aubert et al. 2013) DO produce asymmetric decay from symmetric initial conditions — the asymmetry arises from CMB heat-flux heterogeneity (an INPUT from seismology), not from breaking spherical symmetry at the deepest level. So the dome.+s claim that +asymmetric decay implies asymmetric flow+ is itself ambiguous: standard physics achieves asymmetric decay from a symmetric-rotation core with heterogeneous boundary conditions. The dome conflates +asymmetric flow+ with +broken symmetry at the model level+ when the standard model already has the latter without needing the former.

Quantitative-verification angle (cf. c3 finding): +3:1+ is not a standard CHAOS-7 published quantity. Finlay et al. 2020 publish spherical-harmonic coefficients and secular-variation maps; deriving a +3:1 hemispheric ratio+ requires choosing a specific definition of hemispheric average. The dome doesn.+t specify the definition, so the number itself is undetermined by reference to CHAOS-7. This strengthens the critique: not only is 3:1 not derived from dome geometry, it.+s also not a CHAOS-7-canonical number — making the +matches CHAOS-7+ claim definitionally unfalsifiable until the dome specifies its hemispheric-averaging recipe.
sources: Finlay et al. 2020 CHAOS-7 (doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9) — paper publishes coefficients, not +3:1 ratio+ as a tabulated quantity

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identifies that hemispheric magnetic decay is asymmetric — a real, measured phenomenon (data/wins.json:WIN-005 detail_evidence; raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:13). The structural claim +a symmetric core flow → ~1.0x ratio; observed ratio → 3:1 → core flow must be asymmetric somehow+ is qualitatively correct — fully spherically symmetric MHD does not produce asymmetric decay without something breaking symmetry (boundary conditions, initial conditions, or transport coefficients).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Standard physics already accommodates the asymmetry: Aubert et al. 2013 (data/wins.json:WIN-063 detail_evidence cites doi:10.1038/nature12574) reproduces hemispheric asymmetry via heterogeneous CMB heat flux derived from seismic tomography — symmetric core fluid dynamics + asymmetric boundary conditions = asymmetric decay output. The dome.+s observation that +something must break symmetry+ is true but already answered by standard physics; the dome offers a renamed mechanism (toroidal return-path) without specifying what asymmetry-breaking input produces specifically 3:1, where, or over what timescale. Identifying the puzzle is not solving it (data/wins.json:WIN-063.c3.json:kernel_of_truth, same finding carried forward).

deeper_analysis:
Cycle-3 (2026-05-09) flagged five holes including the (a) numerical-ratio strawman, both TLDR factual errors about +no numerical value+, the unhedged monitor.py absence claim, the over-stated hemispheric-definition critique, and the American-vs-Atlantic wording slip. All five appear addressed in the current text — the rewrite responded substantively to the c3 review. The TLDRs now correctly state the dome +specifies a 3:1 asymmetry ratio+. The monitor.py claim is now hedged. The hemispheric-incommensurability argument is now framed as +central-disc-vs-annulus is structurally asymmetric by construction+. The Atlantic-vs-American wording is fixed.

The rewrite also added net-new material: an explicit advocate-mode pre-empt about Olson&Deguen/Aubert being +fitted not derived+ (anticipating the c3 advocate-mode rebuttal), a SAA-longitude argument against axisymmetric H(r), and a WIN-005/041/042 re-slicing precedent invocation. The advocate-mode pre-empt is good — it eats the rebuttal before it.+s served. The SAA-longitude argument is the strongest single addition. The WIN-041/042 invocation introduces the verdict-tension flagged in Hole 2.

Net read: the c3 → c4 rewrite was substantively responsive. The remaining holes are second-order: source-attribution framing (Hole 1), verdict consistency with invoked precedent (Hole 2), and minor sharpening (Holes 3-4). recommended_action minor_edit, not major_rewrite — the bones are now right; the wording needs another pass.

Meta-observation: this WIN is part of the broader +magnetic decay cluster+ (WIN-040 through WIN-043, WIN-059 through WIN-061, WIN-063) which carries mixed verdicts. Of 8 WINs in the cluster: 5 are +Std Model Explains+, 2 are +Misleading+ (WIN-041/042), 1 is +Self-Contradicted+ (WIN-061). The within-cluster verdict heterogeneity is itself worth a future holistic check — does the verdict assignment reflect a principled distinction or path-dependent review history?

advocate_mode.best_defense: Your c4 review has two structural inconsistencies a dome defender can pivot on. First: your review asserts +the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7 and copied into the WIN-063 paragraph+ — but our WIN-063 trailing paragraph does not cite CHAOS-7 as the source of 3:1. We framed 3:1 as a structural prediction of toroidal asymmetric flow versus a symmetric globe dipole. You.+re attributing a source to us we never claimed; that.+s a strawman by your own internal-consistency-first principle. Second: your own paragraph calls this +the same pattern that earned WIN-041 and WIN-042 Misleading verdicts+ — yet you grade WIN-063 +Std Model Explains+, not +Misleading+. By your own re-slicing precedent, WIN-063 should be Misleading. Pick a lane. Once these two are fixed, the remaining critique reduces to +you didn.+t derive 3:1 from dome geometry+ — which is true, but standard physics also doesn.+t derive 3:1 from first principles either; Aubert et al. 2013 reproduces asymmetric decay via CMB heat-flux inputs from seismic tomography, which is itself observational. Both models adopt observational inputs; the question of which is +really+ deriving is itself ambiguous.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Three edits in priority order: (1) Replace +read directly off CHAOS-7+ with dilemma framing — +either adopted from observation (post-hoc) or asserted by fiat (no derivation); either path fails the independent-prediction test+; (2) Reconcile WIN-041/042 precedent invocation with the Std Model Explains verdict — preferred approach: add a sentence noting WIN-041 was Misleading for station-counting and WIN-042 for threshold-duplication while WIN-063 is in the same spirit but a distinct operational pattern, and Std Model Explains applies because the underlying phenomenon is independently explained by reversed-flux geodynamo; (3) Pre-empt the chain-of-fitting rebuttal by adding one sentence: +Both standard physics and the dome adopt observational inputs, but standard physics chains them across independent domains (seismic data → CMB boundary conditions → MHD output → magnetic observations), while the dome reads the answer directly off the magnetic-decay data with no independent input domain. The chain length is the methodological discriminator.+

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Verdict-vs-precedent tension. c4 detail_evidence text invokes WIN-041/042 +Misleading+ verdicts as precedent for the WIN-063 re-slicing pattern, but WIN-063 itself is verdict +Std Model Explains+. The cluster as a whole has mixed verdicts (5 Std Model / 2 Misleading / 1 Self-Contradicted) which may reflect path-dependent review history rather than principled distinction. See Hole 2 for the recommended reconciliation. Beyond this, no other cross-WIN argumentation contradictions found — Atlantic-vs-American wording is now consistent with WIN-005, Olson&Deguen citations are unique to WIN-063, and post-hoc framing follows the pattern established in WIN-041/042/004/005 detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraphs.

quantitative_verification.computations: Direct character-length count of current data/wins.json:WIN-063 fields, jq pipeline for verdict tally, grep on raw-text for dome.+s verbatim phrasing. The c3 quantitative_verification finding (+3:1 is not a standard CHAOS-7 published quantity+) flowed correctly into c4 detail_evidence framing but introduced the source-attribution strawman noted in Hole 1.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: c4 detail_evidence asserts the dome +adopts that ratio from CHAOS-7 observation+ and +the 3:1 is read directly off CHAOS-7+ — but the dome paragraph does not cite CHAOS-7 as the source. Either the dome adopted 3:1 from CHAOS-7 (which would be post-hoc but is unattested in the dome paragraph), or the dome asserted 3:1 by fiat (which is undermotivated but also unattested). Our text picks the first interpretation without textual support.
